description: §42.3.5 / §42.3.6 absence-safe render, ABSENT path. The plain-optional cell is `not`. `${@user?.name}` (optional-chain, §42.3.6) short-circuits to `not` and renders NOTHING (no-value-no-output, §6), so #opt reads only "Name:". `if=@user` is falsy so the guard is OFF (#guard `display: none`) and the guarded `${@user.name}` interp does NOT run — #inner is empty (vs "Ada" in the -render twin). Both forms compile clean (no E-TYPE-046). Twin of optional-member-access-render — the empty/fallback render.
rationale: §42.3.6: `recv?.field` short-circuits to `not` when the receiver is `not`; §6 no-value-no-output renders nothing. §42.4: `not` is falsy, so `if=@user` unmounts the guarded subtree. Absence renders as nothing, never the string "undefined" (§42.8).
